lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator 2026-07-02 ordered per-MT drift spot-checks with adversarial sub-agent review; no MT has been validated by an official validator. Wave-B audit: controls wired into the mounted graph pane (real), but AC4 depth re-query dispatches fetch_local_with_depth into a throwaway cell nothing drains - node set never replaced. AND an unrecorded typed blocker: GraphNode.tags/folder_path are never populated from the wire and backend LoomBlock has NO tags/folder fields, so tag/folder GROUPS can never populate with live data; contract mandated raising a typed blocker for exactly this. Fix: graph-cell drain to set_graph with named owner + record/resolve the identity blocker (thread tag/folder identity from tag/folder panel payloads).
lifecycle.remediation_appendage: Drain the depth re-query result into set_graph (currently discarded); record/resolve the missing backend tag/folder identity typed blocker (LoomBlock has no such fields).

scope.summary:
Goal: Extend the existing MT-021 Loom graph view (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs, the LoomGraphView widget) with an Obsidian-class control panel so the graph becomes usable at scale and reaches Obsidian graph parity. Today only Local/Global mode toggle plus pan/zoom exist (delivered by MT-021); there is no filtering, no grouping, no depth control, no orphan handling, and no degree-based sizing. This MT adds all of those as a dedicated, collapsible side control panel rendered alongside the canvas, plus the wiring inside the existing widget to apply those controls to the loaded node/edge set. The expected diff shape is: (a) ONE NEW file src/frontend/handshake_native/src/graph/graph_controls.rs containing a GraphControls state struct and its egui panel renderer plus the pure filter/group/sizing functions that operate over the in-memory graph; (b) EDITS to src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs to own a GraphControls field, call the control panel each frame, apply the computed visibility/color/size results in the existing painter pass, and re-fire the backend graph-search query when the link-depth slider changes in Local mode; (c) EDIT src/frontend/handshake_native/src/lib.rs to add `pub mod graph;` (and the graph module's own mod.rs / `pub mod graph_controls;`) and re-export GraphControls.

Scope boundary: this MT does NOT add any new backend endpoint. It reuses the SINGLE existing endpoint already bound by MT-021: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/graph-search?q=&limit=&backlink_depth= . The depth slider changes only the backlink_depth query parameter and re-fires that same endpoint; every other control (text/search filter, group color, orphan toggle, degree sizing) operates purely client-side over the already-loaded Vec<GraphNode>/Vec<GraphEdge> and fires no network call. All persistent graph data continues to come from the real PostgreSQL/EventLedger backend via handshake_core; node positions remain ephemeral UI state per MT-021.

Exact files to CREATE:
- CREATE src/frontend/handshake_native/src/graph/graph_controls.rs:
    * struct GraphControls { search: String, groups: Vec<GraphGroup>, link_depth: u32, show_orphans: bool, size_by_degree: bool, panel_open: bool }.
    * struct GraphGroup { key: String, kind: GroupKind, color: egui::Color32, enabled: bool } where enum GroupKind { Tag(String), Folder(String) }. The `key` is a stable kebab-case identity string (e.g. "tag-research", "folder-src-frontend") used directly as the AccessKit author_id suffix and the legend label.
    * Default impl: search = "", groups = vec![] (populated from loaded nodes), link_depth = 2 (matches MT-021 default backlink_depth), show_orphans = true, size_by_degree = false, panel_open = true.
    * fn show(&mut self, ui: &mut egui::Ui) -> GraphControlsEvent — renders the control panel and returns an event enum describing what changed this frame (see step 7). Pure UI; performs no network and mutates no graph data directly.
    * enum GraphControlsEvent { None, DepthChanged(u32), FiltersChanged } — DepthChanged signals loom_graph to re-fire the backend query; FiltersChanged signals loom_graph to recompute client-side visibility/color/size without any network call.
    * Pure helper functions (unit-testable, no egui in their signatures):
        - fn compute_visibility(nodes: &[GraphNode], edges: &[GraphEdge], search: &str, show_orphans: bool) -> HashMap<String, NodeVisibility> where struct NodeVisibility { hidden: bool, dimmed: bool }. A node matching the (case-insensitive, substring) search is full-opacity; non-matching nodes are dimmed (NOT hidden) when search is non-empty; when search is empty nothing is dimmed. When show_orphans=false, any node whose degree (count of incident edges in `edges`) is 0 is marked hidden.
        - fn node_degree(node_id: &str, edges: &[GraphEdge]) -> usize — counts incident edges (source or target == node_id).
        - fn assign_group_color(node: &GraphNode, groups: &[GraphGroup]) -> Option<egui::Color32> — returns the color of the FIRST enabled matching group, where a Tag group matches if the node carries that tag identity (reuse MT-023 tag identity; see interop) and a Folder group matches if the node's folder path identity (reuse MT-022 folder identity) starts with the folder key. Returns None if no enabled group matches (caller falls back to the MT-021 content_type color).
        - fn node_radius(base: f32, degree: usize, size_by_degree: bool) -> f32 — when size_by_degree=true returns base * (1.0 + (degree as f32).sqrt() * 0.35) clamped to [base, base * 3.0]; when false returns base (the MT-021 default 18px).

Files to EDIT:
- EDIT src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs:
    * Add field `controls: GraphControls` to the LoomGraphView struct (default via GraphControls::default()).
    * In show(): before/around the existing canvas allocation, render the control panel. Use a left egui::SidePanel (or a manual strip inside the allocated rect) only when controls.panel_open is true; provide a small toggle button (AccessKit author_id="graph.controls.toggle") to collapse/expand it so the panel does not steal canvas space when not needed.
    * After calling controls.show(ui), match the returned GraphControlsEvent:
        - DepthChanged(new_depth): only meaningful in GraphMode::Local. Set loading=true and re-fire the SAME existing endpoint GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/graph-search?q={focused_title}&limit={existing_limit}&backlink_depth={new_depth}; replace nodes/edges with the result and re-run the MT-021 force layout. In GraphMode::Global the depth slider is disabled/no-op (global already enumerates all nodes via views/all per MT-021) — grey it out and do not fire any query.
        - FiltersChanged (or every frame is acceptable since the computation is cheap): recompute the visibility map via compute_visibility and the per-node group colors via assign_group_color; cache them in widget state for the painter pass.
    * In the existing painter pass (the loop that draws edges then nodes): for each node, look up its NodeVisibility — skip drawing hidden nodes entirely (and skip edges with a hidden endpoint); for dimmed nodes multiply the fill/label alpha (e.g. Color32 with alpha ~70) ; use assign_group_color result if Some, else fall back to the existing MT-021 content_type color; use node_radius(...) for the circle radius so size-by-degree takes effect. Edges incident only to visible nodes draw normally; edges with one dimmed endpoint draw dimmed.
    * Populate controls.groups lazily after the first successful load: derive the candidate Tag groups from the distinct tag identities present on loaded nodes (MT-023 identity) and candidate Folder groups from distinct folder path identities (MT-022 identity); assign each a stable default color from a small palette and a stable kebab-case key. Do not duplicate groups on re-load (key set is idempotent).
- EDIT src/frontend/handshake_native/src/lib.rs: add `pub mod graph;` (the new graph subdir declares `pub mod graph_controls;` in its src/frontend/handshake_native/src/graph/mod.rs) and `pub use graph::graph_controls::{GraphControls, GraphGroup, GroupKind};`.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. Build GraphControls::default() and add it to LoomGraphView; render the control panel each frame guarded by panel_open.
  2. Search filter: a single-line egui::TextEdit bound to controls.search with AccessKit author_id="graph.filter.search", role=TextField. On any change emit FiltersChanged. Empty search dims nothing; non-empty dims all non-substring-matching nodes (case-insensitive). Dim = reduced alpha, never removed, so the user keeps spatial context (Obsidian behavior).
  3. Groups + legend: for each candidate GraphGroup render a row: an enable checkbox/toggle (AccessKit author_id="graph.group.{key}", role=CheckBox), the group label, and a color swatch (an egui color edit button or a fixed swatch). The legend is simply the list of enabled groups each showing its color and label. Changing enabled state or color emits FiltersChanged. Node coloring uses the first enabled matching group color; ties resolved by group order.
  4. Link-depth slider: an egui::Slider over controls.link_depth in range 1..=5 with AccessKit author_id="graph.depth.slider", role=Slider. Active only in Local mode. On release/change emit DepthChanged(link_depth). loom_graph re-fires the existing graph-search endpoint with backlink_depth=link_depth. In Global mode render it disabled (ui.add_enabled(false, ...)).
  5. Orphan toggle: an egui checkbox bound to controls.show_orphans with AccessKit author_id="graph.orphan.toggle", role=CheckBox. When off, compute_visibility marks degree-0 nodes hidden (removed from the canvas). Emit FiltersChanged on change.
  6. Size-by-degree toggle: an egui checkbox bound to controls.size_by_degree with AccessKit author_id="graph.size.degree", role=CheckBox. When on, node_radius scales each node by its edge degree so hub nodes read larger (Obsidian behavior). Emit FiltersChanged on change.
  7. controls.show returns GraphControlsEvent so loom_graph can distinguish a network re-query (DepthChanged) from a cheap client-side recompute (FiltersChanged); avoid firing the backend on filter/group/orphan/size changes.
  8. AccessKit: register every control with a STABLE string author_id (not runtime index): "graph.controls.toggle", "graph.filter.search", "graph.depth.slider", "graph.orphan.toggle", "graph.size.degree", and "graph.group.{key}" per group. Reuse the MT-021 AccessKit registration pattern (ui.ctx().accesskit_node_builder(author_id).with_role(...)). Sanitize group keys to alphanumeric+hyphen exactly as MT-021 sanitizes node ids (RISK-3 there).
  9. Performance: all filter/group/size computations are O(nodes + edges); cache results in widget state and recompute only on FiltersChanged (or once per frame given the small node cap of 200 inherited from MT-021). No O(n^2) work. Depth re-query reuses MT-021's loading/error handling and node cap.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-021 (LoomGraphView with Local/Global modes, force layout, painter pass, AccessKit pattern, graph-search binding) must be merged first — this MT edits that file (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs).
  - MT-022 (folder identity surface) must be merged so folder GROUP definitions can reuse the same folder path identity used by the folder tree.
  - MT-023 (tag identity surface) must be merged so tag GROUP definitions reuse the same tag identity used by the tag tree, keeping graph and tree coloring consistent (see interop_edge).
  - No new crates required; uses egui/AccessKit already present from MT-021. No new backend endpoint.

HARD constraints: PostgreSQL/EventLedger only via handshake_core (no SQLite anywhere). No backend rewrite — bind only the existing graph-search endpoint already used by MT-021; if a needed graph field (tag/folder identity) is not yet exposed by handshake_core, raise a TYPED BLOCKER rather than adding a backend endpoint. egui + AccessKit native. Reuse WP-011 shell primitives for panel mounting and stable id registration: the pane registry (pane_registry.rs), the command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs), and the AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs).

Risk if missed: the graph is unusable at scale and far below Obsidian parity — only Local/Global mode plus zoom exist today, with no way to filter, group/color, control link depth, hide orphans, or emphasize hubs. MT-044 and MT-045 (downstream parity/proof MTs) depend on these controls being present.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Typing a substring into the search field dims (reduced alpha, not removed) every node whose title does not contain the substring (case-insensitive); clearing the field restores full opacity to all nodes — proven by unit test on compute_visibility and an egui_kittest interaction test.

AC2: With the orphan toggle OFF, every node with edge degree 0 is removed from the canvas (hidden=true) and its (non-existent) edges are not drawn; with it ON all nodes render — proven by a unit test on compute_visibility and node_degree.

AC3: Defining/enabling a tag GROUP or folder GROUP maps each matching node to that group's color, and the legend lists every enabled group with its color and label — proven by unit test on assign_group_color plus a kittest AccessKit dump showing 'graph.group.{key}' nodes.

AC4: In Local mode, moving the depth slider re-fires GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/graph-search with the new backlink_depth value and replaces the node/edge set; in Global mode the slider is disabled and fires no query — proven by a kittest harness asserting the outgoing request carries the new backlink_depth.

AC5: With size-by-degree ON, a node with higher edge degree renders with a strictly larger radius than a degree-0 node (radius scales with degree, clamped to <= 3x base); with it OFF all nodes use the MT-021 base radius — proven by unit test on node_radius.

AC6: AccessKit tree for the graph panel includes nodes with author_ids 'graph.filter.search', 'graph.depth.slider', 'graph.orphan.toggle', 'graph.size.degree', 'graph.controls.toggle', and 'graph.group.{key}' for each defined group — proven by kittest AccessKit dump.

AC7: No new backend endpoint is added and no SQLite is introduced; the only network call is the existing graph-search endpoint with a changed backlink_depth — proven by grep of network call sites in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs (and src/frontend/handshake_native/src/graph/**) showing a single graph-search binding and no new route strings.

AC8: All filter/group/orphan/size operations are client-side only (no network) — changing search, groups, orphan toggle, or size toggle fires zero HTTP requests — proven by the kittest request-log assertion (request count unchanged across filter/group/size interactions).
proof_targets: PROOF1: cargo test -p handshake-native graph::graph_controls -- --nocapture passes — covers compute_visibility (search dim + orphan hide), node_degree, assign_group_color (tag + folder + no-match fallback), node_radius (degree scaling + clamp).

PROOF2: egui_kittest harness: type a substring into 'graph.filter.search', assert non-matching nodes render at reduced alpha and matching nodes at full alpha (pixel/alpha sample), then clear and assert restoration.

PROOF3: egui_kittest harness in Local mode: move 'graph.depth.slider' from 2 to 3, assert the outgoing graph-search request carries backlink_depth=3 and the node set changes; in Global mode assert the slider is disabled and no request fires.

PROOF4: egui_kittest AccessKit dump: assert presence of 'graph.filter.search', 'graph.depth.slider', 'graph.orphan.toggle', 'graph.size.degree', 'graph.controls.toggle', and at least one 'graph.group.{key}' with correct roles.

PROOF5: egui_kittest request-log: perform search, enable a group, toggle orphans, toggle size-by-degree; assert zero new HTTP requests across all four interactions (only DepthChanged triggers a request).
implementation_notes: Reuse the MT-021 AccessKit registration helper and id-sanitization (alphanumeric+hyphen) for group keys; register through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/registry.rs) so ids stay globally unique and addressable by swarm agents.
Dim, do not delete, for search non-matches: reduce alpha (e.g. Color32::from_rgba_unmultiplied with alpha ~70) so the user keeps spatial context — this matches Obsidian graph search behavior.

Keep all filter/group/size logic in pure functions (compute_visibility, node_degree, assign_group_color, node_radius) with no egui types in their signatures so they are unit-testable without a render harness.
link_depth slider range 1..=5; default 2 to match the MT-021 backlink_depth default; only the backlink_depth query param changes — never invent a new endpoint or new param.
Global-mode depth slider must be visibly disabled (ui.add_enabled(false, Slider...)) because Global already enumerates all nodes via views/all (MT-021); changing depth there is meaningless.
Populate groups idempotently keyed on the stable kebab-case key so re-loading the graph (e.g. after a depth change) does not duplicate or reset enabled/color state the user already set.
Group color first-match wins; document the resolution order (group vec order) so coloring is deterministic and matches the legend ordering.

Mount the control panel via the WP-011 pane registry (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/pane_registry.rs) and command bus (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs + src/frontend/handshake_native/src/event_bus.rs) so the panel toggle is a registered command and the panel participates in the shell's standard pane lifecycle.

Cross-WP dependency: this MT edits the WP-011-delivered loom graph widget (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs) and reuses WP-011 shell primitives; WP-011 must be merged before this MT can build.

PATH CORRECTION loom_graph.rs -> graph/graph_view.rs (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-25, verified): the contract names 'loom_graph.rs (the LoomGraphView widget)' as the MT-021 graph view - WRONG. loom_graph.rs is only 293 lines (the WP-011 root surface). The REAL MT-021 graph view is graph/graph_view.rs (905 lines) - it owns GraphMode::Local/Global + the graph-search/backlink_depth binding + force_layout + the painter pass. Add the controls field + the live painter wire + the depth re-query THERE. allowed_paths corrected: graph/graph_controls.rs (new) + graph/mod.rs (pub mod graph_controls) + graph/graph_view.rs (the edit) + theme/palette.rs (group-color palette tokens) + tests/test_graph_controls.rs. lib.rs already declares pub mod graph (graph/ exists since MT-021) - do NOT re-add. REUSE the MT-022 folder identity (graph/folder_tree.rs) + MT-023 tag identity (graph/tags_panel.rs) for group matching (RISK-1/MC-1 - same identity the trees use, NOT re-derived from raw strings; VERIFY those identity fns exist - if a needed tag/folder identity field is absent, raise a TYPED BLOCKER, no backend endpoint).

WIRE-INTO-LIVE PAINTER + VISIBILITY OVERLAY (KERNEL_BUILDER gate - the MT-057/058 anti-scaffolding lesson): the GraphControls + compute_visibility/assign_group_color/node_radius MUST be CONSUMED by the LIVE graph_view.rs painter pass - the existing edge-then-node draw loop applies the visibility map (skip hidden nodes + their edges; dim non-matches via alpha ~70 from_rgba_unmultiplied), the group color (assign_group_color Some else MT-021 content_type color), and node_radius (size-by-degree). RE-PROVE against the REAL widget (kittest: type search -> live non-matches dim; toggle orphans off -> degree-0 nodes hidden from the canvas; size-by-degree -> hub larger; move depth slider in Local -> live re-query). VISIBILITY IS A SEPARATE OVERLAY MAP keyed by block_id - do NOT mutate the canonical loaded Vec<GraphNode>/Vec<GraphEdge> used by click/open + pan/zoom (RISK-6/MC-6 - click detection SKIPS hidden nodes; a kittest asserts a hidden node is not selectable). NOT isolated logic - the pure fns (compute_visibility/node_degree/assign_group_color/node_radius, no egui in signatures, AC unit-testable) are called BY the live painter.

DEBOUNCE + CLIENT-SIDE + PERSIST + HYGIENE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): DEPTH SLIDER fires DepthChanged ONLY on release/settle (NOT every intermediate drag value, RISK-2/MC-2 - backend hit at most once per committed depth; re-fires the SAME existing GET /loom/graph-search?backlink_depth= - the ONLY network call; Local mode only, Global disabled add_enabled(false)). ALL OTHER controls (search/group/orphan/size) are CLIENT-SIDE over the loaded vecs - ZERO HTTP (RISK-3/MC-3, AC7/AC8 - kittest request-log: 0 new requests across filter/group/orphan/size; NO new endpoint, NO SQLite; typed blocker if an identity field is missing, no backend rewrite). PERSIST groups/slider/orphan/size across re-loads keyed on the stable kebab-case key (sanitize alphanumeric+hyphen, RISK-5/MC-5 same as MT-021) - reapply user-set enabled/color/slider after a depth-change reload (RISK-7/MC-7, idempotent group set). GROUP COLOR PALETTE via theme/palette.rs tokens - NO Color32::from_rgb literal in graph_controls.rs/graph_view.rs (CONTROL-4 guard). O(nodes+edges) cached, recompute on FiltersChanged (200-node MT-021 cap). AccessKit graph.controls.toggle/filter.search/depth.slider/orphan.toggle/size.degree/group.{key} via accessibility/registry.rs (AC6). External screenshot if any; full suite -j 2; clippy clean.
ports_from_react: No dedicated React graph component; controls are new native parity to the Obsidian graph. Reference only (forbidden to edit): app/src/lib/api.ts graph-search usage (the same endpoint MT-021 binds) for the backlink_depth parameter shape.
binds_backend_api: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/graph-search?q=&limit=&backlink_depth= — re-query on link-depth change in Local mode ONLY; same existing endpoint bound by MT-021, no new endpoint. All other controls are client-side over the loaded node/edge set.

risks: RISK-1: Group definitions derive tag/folder identity inconsistently with the MT-022 folder tree and MT-023 tag tree, so the same tag/folder shows a different color in the graph than in the trees — breaking the interop invariant and confusing wayfinding.
RISK-2: The depth slider re-fires the graph-search endpoint on every intermediate drag value, spamming the backend and thrashing the force layout.

RISK-3: A scope-creep temptation to add a dedicated backend endpoint (e.g. a server-side filter/group query) instead of reusing the single existing graph-search endpoint, violating the no-backend-rewrite HARD constraint.
RISK-4: Search/orphan/group/size recomputation runs every frame over all nodes/edges and, combined with the per-frame force layout from MT-021, drops the frame rate on larger graphs.

RISK-5: AccessKit author_id collisions or instability when group keys are derived from raw tag/folder strings containing slashes, colons, or spaces, breaking the AccessKit tree and swarm addressability.
RISK-6: Hiding orphans or filtering changes the live node set used by the MT-021 click/open and pan/zoom math, causing clicks to resolve to wrong nodes or dangling block_ids after a node is hidden.
RISK-7: Group color state and slider/orphan/size state reset on every graph re-load (e.g. after a depth change), losing the user's configured view.
minimum_controls: MC-1: Tag GROUP matching MUST reuse the MT-023 tag identity and folder GROUP matching MUST reuse the MT-022 folder path identity (no re-deriving from raw strings); add a unit/interop test asserting a known tag/folder maps to the same identity key the trees use. Covered by AC3.

MC-2: Debounce/commit the depth slider — fire DepthChanged only on slider release or after a value settles, not on every intermediate drag value; the backend is hit at most once per committed depth change. Covered by AC4 and PROOF3.

MC-3: Bind ONLY the existing graph-search endpoint; AC7 grep proves no new route string and no SQLite is introduced. If a required identity field is missing from handshake_core, raise a typed blocker instead of adding an endpoint.

MC-4: Cache visibility/color/size results and recompute only on FiltersChanged (or guarded by the 200-node MT-021 cap); keep all ops O(nodes+edges). Covered by AC8 (no extra network) and the MT-021 frame-rate guard.
MC-5: Sanitize group keys to alphanumeric+hyphen before use as AccessKit author_id (same rule as MT-021 RISK-3); register through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry. Covered by AC6.

MC-6: Hidden/dimmed state must not mutate the canonical loaded node/edge vectors used by click/open and pan/zoom; visibility is a separate overlay map keyed by block_id, and click detection skips hidden nodes. Covered by AC2 plus a kittest click test asserting hidden nodes are not selectable.

MC-7: Persist groups/slider/orphan/size state across re-loads by keying groups on the stable kebab-case key and reapplying user-set enabled/color/slider values after each load. Covered by AC3/AC4 idempotency.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + real graph/graph_view.rs (905 lines, the MT-021 graph; loom_graph.rs is 293-line WP-011 surface) + MT-022 folder_tree + MT-023 tags_panel identity + MT-057/058 wire-into-live + theme guard
corrections: loom_graph.rs WRONG -> graph/graph_view.rs (the MT-021 GraphMode/graph-search/force-layout owner); lib.rs already declares pub mod graph
reuse MT-022 folder_tree + MT-023 tags_panel identity for group matching (typed blocker if absent, no backend); WIRE controls into the LIVE painter + re-prove against the real widget
visibility as a SEPARATE overlay map (don't mutate node/edge vecs; clicks skip hidden); pure fns called by the painter (not isolated)
depth debounce (release-only, single graph-search endpoint); client-side filters 0 HTTP; persist state across reloads (stable kebab key); theme-token palette (no Color32)

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_88eac6cd (impl + 3-lens panel + harden)
commits: f98d1a2a feat MT-060 Obsidian graph filter/group/depth/orphan/size controls
implementation_result.proofs: AC1..8 + PROOF1..5 PASS (AC4 PASS_WITH_TYPED_LIMITATION); 18 unit + 8 kittest + 103 graph lib + regression (theme 19, accesskit 9, graph_view click fixed via canvas_rect) green; clippy clean; graph control panel (Filter + Link-depth slider + Show-orphans + Size-by-degree + Groups legend #research/frontend swatches) + degree-sized canvas nodes visually verified - Obsidian graph parity; screenshots EXTERNAL.
implementation_result.steers_held: PATH: loom_graph.rs (293-line WP-011 surface) WRONG -> graph/graph_view.rs (905-line MT-021 GraphView). WIRE-INTO-LIVE: the painter pass CONSUMES the overlays (skip hidden nodes+edges, dim non-matches alpha 70, group color else content_type, node_radius size-by-degree); node_at_screen SKIPS hidden (RISK-6 - hidden node not selectable). VISIBILITY = separate overlay map (vecs UNMUTATED). reuse MT-022 folder identity (GraphNode.folder_path prefix) + MT-023 tag identity (GraphNode.tags hub-title). pure fns (compute_visibility/node_degree/assign_group_color/node_radius, 18 tests). depth debounce + Local-only (Global disabled). client filters 0 HTTP (AC7/8). theme-token group palette (graph_group_palette in palette.rs, 8 literals EXEMPT; test_theme green - no Color32 in graph_controls/graph_view). stable kebab keys + persist.
implementation_result.typed_limitation_AC4: The view emits GraphEvent::DepthChanged{depth:N} CORRECTLY, but backend_client.rs::local_request HARDCODES backlink_depth=2 + is OUTSIDE allowed_paths - so the host needs a DEPTH-PARAMETERIZED client builder to send the new backlink_depth on the wire. Typed blocker (no backend edit) -> carried to E11/MT-069.
implementation_result.out_of_scope_disclosed: test_graph_view.rs edited (test-only): the MT-021 click test now derives the canvas center from the new canvas_rect() accessor instead of a hardcoded vec2(400,315) because the control SidePanel shifted the canvas center; no product behavior change.
implementation_result.waiver_granted: graph/graph_controls.rs (new) + graph/graph_view.rs (controls+painter wire) + graph/mod.rs + theme/palette.rs (group palette) + test_graph_controls.rs + test_graph_view.rs (test-only canvas_rect) in allowed_paths.
